content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates consistent, specific, and timely reads throughout. In turn 2, they catch the linguistic frame of the opening question itself ('that question is interesting'). In turn 4, they mirror back the precise language ('static that got turned off') and direct attention to the body. Turn 6 contains a sharp read of the client's refusal-to-perform as itself the most honest signal — naming a stance, not just content. Turn 8 reads the reflexive quality of 'I'm fine' before asking about it. Most critically, turn 10 reads both what was said and the retraction maneuver around it, refusing to take the out — and turn 12 names that pattern explicitly ('giving people the retraction so neither of you has to deal with it — that's been happening for a while, hasn't it'), elevating it from moment to pattern. Reads consistently precede questions and are specific (linguistic, somatic, relational-pattern) rather than generic. The rush signature isn't explicitly named but the functional equivalent — the troubleshooting/managing stance — is tracked from turn 2 onward.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the framing of the client's own question as data — notices the diagnostic stance implicit in how it's asked."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Mirrors precise language ('static that got turned off') and pivots from content to somatic location — read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Excellent stance-level read: names the refusal-to-perform as the most honest thing said. This is a specific AoA-flavored read of how the client is showing up, not what they're saying."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads 'I'm fine' as a move worth inquiring into rather than taking it at face value — distinguishing said from shown."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads both the disclosure and the retraction maneuver in real time, names that the retraction won't be taken, and shifts to safety frame appropriately."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern-level read: names the retraction-offering as a longstanding pattern, not just a moment. Read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the weight of what was said ('took something to say') and tracks the isolation ('not a small thing to carry alone') before moving to the resource question."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "expected_territory_misses": [],
  "notes": "The troubleshooting/managing stance the coach catches in turn 2 functions as a rush-signature-adjacent read (managing-as-pace). Reads are specific and timely throughout; the coach repeatedly grounds questions in what was noticed."
}
